stability_gate.quantization_tolerance_policy: The historical one-frame 95-to-96 correction remains a closed measurement decision; it is not iterated.
stability_gate.session_band_correction: Owner-approved on 2026-07-17 from a complete twelve-cycle curve, not from an isolated overrun. Values above 98 frames stop again; the band is not widened iteratively.
stability_gate.meaning: The gate proves that the measured 95-to-98-frame distribution has not regressed. Success is LIMITATION-STABLE, never a performance PASS.

user_documentation.exact_limit: On the reference MEGA65, the first expression compiled after a persistent definition typically takes 1.90 to 1.96 seconds; occasionally longer times have been observed. Immediately following warm expressions take about 0.20 seconds.
user_documentation.usage_hint: Enter related definitions as a block before testing them so one compiler reload is amortized across the block.

cure.relationship_to_ship_builder: C2 and the on-device ship builder are the two committed 1.2 lead themes; their ordering and scope split remain a later owner decision.

renewal.trigger: If C2 leaves the 1.2 scope, this exception expires and requires a new explicit owner decision.

decision_basis.resident_compiler: rejected: misses the EXT floor by 643 bytes
decision_basis.identity_bound_restore: rejected: l65m-commit and C1-lifetime slice overruns plus runtime-overlay-bank deficit
decision_basis.rollback_cost: would unwind approximately 9.2 KiB of structural EXT relief plus filter and the banner

claim_limit: This is a dated product exception, not a changed performance target and not a PASS. It permits C1 promotion only while the exact limitation, batching guidance, C2/1.2 cure and non-automatic renewal rule remain bound by the product documentation and gate receipts.
